Rule Title: Answer early with a citable lead
Rule Statement: Place the most decision-useful answer or takeaway in the opening section of the page.
Why It Matters: Cited sentences in Google AI Mode skew toward the top portion of pages.
Applies To: Explainers, market pages, informational landing pages
Does Not Apply To: Narrative-first essays or pages where suspense is part of the format
Implementation Pattern: Open with 2-4 lines that resolve the query before expanding context.
Before Example: First 4 paragraphs wander through scene-setting before giving the answer.
After Example: The opening paragraph states the conclusion, scope and key qualifier clearly.
Success Metric: Higher citation capture; lower pogo risk; clearer snippet extraction

Rule Statement: Write important claims as short, self-contained factual sentences.
Why It Matters: Short standalone claims are easier for systems to extract, cite and trust.
Does Not Apply To: Highly literary brand pages where style outweighs extractability
Implementation Pattern: Break compound claims into separate sentences; avoid burying facts inside long clauses.
Before Example: Because demand is rising across several use cases and markets, the category is benefiting from structural adoption and strategic investment across channels.
After Example: Demand is rising in industrial automation. Investment is following adoption in adjacent workflows.
Success Metric: More quote-like extractability; better sentence-level matching

Rule Statement: Use headings, subheads, lists, tables and explicit sections when they improve scanability.
Why It Matters: Structured pages are easier for retrieval and sentence matching than undifferentiated prose.
Applies To: Guides, FAQs, market explainers, comparison pages
Does Not Apply To: Minimalist opinion essays with a deliberate long-form style
Implementation Pattern: Add meaningful sectioning and explicit topical containers.
After Example: Clear H2/H3 sequence plus a short table or FAQ block where useful.
Success Metric: Higher section retrieval; easier auditing; faster human consumption

Rule Statement: Design pages and clusters to answer adjacent sub-questions, not only the head keyword.
Why It Matters: AI systems expand prompts into related sub-queries before selecting sources.
Applies To: Topic clusters, category hubs, research libraries
Does Not Apply To: Single-purpose utility pages where adjacent coverage would distract
Implementation Pattern: Map likely follow-up questions and cover them across linked pages or sections.
Before Example: One page targets only the exact keyword phrase and ignores adjacent demand.
After Example: The page and its linked cluster cover core query, use cases, comparisons, and objections.
Success Metric: Greater citation footprint across query neighborhood

Rule Title: Check crawler eligibility before content diagnosis
Rule Statement: Verify crawl and index controls for the AI surface you care about before diagnosing content weakness.
Why It Matters: A blocked crawler cannot cite or surface content regardless of quality.
Does Not Apply To: Cases where the page is intentionally blocked for legal or strategic reasons
Implementation Pattern: Run a technical preflight: robots directives, indexability, canonicalization and analytics tagging.
Before Example: The team rewrites copy for a page that OAI-SearchBot is blocked from accessing.
After Example: The team first confirms crawler access, then optimizes content.

Rule Statement: Do not publish pages that merely remix widely available information without adding value.
Why It Matters: Official Google guidance emphasizes unique, non-commodity helpful content.
Applies To: SEO content, research pages, thought leadership with search intent
Does Not Apply To: Purely internal documents not meant for search discovery
Implementation Pattern: Add original analysis, synthesis, examples, data, or a distinct point of view.
Before Example: The page paraphrases five competitor pages and adds no new interpretation.
After Example: The page adds a proprietary framework, sharper comparison or firsthand synthesis.
Success Metric: Better differentiation; lower risk of sameness

Rule Statement: Track citations, assisted visits, branded searches and AI referral traffic, not only organic clicks.
Why It Matters: AI surfaces can change click patterns while still shaping awareness and downstream demand.
Applies To: Search reporting and content performance analysis
Does Not Apply To: Very narrow direct-response pages where only last-click matters
Implementation Pattern: Add separate dashboards for ChatGPT referrals, cited URLs and assisted conversions.
Before Example: Monthly reporting only tracks Google organic sessions and rankings.
After Example: Reporting includes citations, AI referrals and influenced pipeline metrics.

Rule Statement: Separate rules for discoverability, citation selection and answer inclusion.
Why It Matters: Optimization tactics can help one stage and hurt another under realistic pipelines.
Does Not Apply To: Tiny one-page experiments where stage separation adds unnecessary complexity
Implementation Pattern: Tag each best practice by pipeline stage and review impact separately.
Before Example: One generic checklist is applied to every page and every platform.
After Example: Rules are grouped by crawl/discoverability, retrieval, citation and response fit.
Success Metric: Cleaner experimentation and fewer false conclusions
